symptoms.symptom_name: Erythema, warmth and swelling over the implant site

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The cardinal features of cellulitis; over a retained implant they point to an infected foreign-body nidus.
Redness alone is non-specific; without fever/induration it could be superficial.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe throbbing pain markedly worse on arm movement, with guarding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep throbbing pain indicates true soft-tissue infection rather than a surface reaction; guarding reflects the inflamed nidus.
Pain grossly out of proportion is a hallmark of necrotising infection; here pain is severe but proportionate, no crepitus/bullae — argues against.

symptoms.symptom_name: Feverish and shivery sensation (early systemic feature)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Systemic response accompanying local infection; raises urgency toward same-day in-person source control.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable retained subdermal implant beneath the inflamed skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The retained, expired foreign body is the infective focus and the reason antibiotics alone are insufficient — it mandates source control.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of rigors, confusion, rapidly tracking erythema or haemorrhagic bullae

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Their absence keeps the case off the emergency (T4/T5) pathway and supports urgent same-day in-person management with a conservative safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals — low-grade fever 37.8 degrees C and mild tachycardia HR 105

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective early systemic response — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers systemic involvement from the patient's feverish/shivery report, not a measured value.

symptoms.symptom_name: Local exam findings — 4 cm area of induration and warmth with exquisite tenderness, no purulent fluctuance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable induration and warmth confirm true cellulitis; absence of fluctuance suggests no drainable abscess yet — assessable only by hands-on examination, hence sealed.

investigation_details.investigation_name: Wound swab / tissue culture at the time of in-person source control
investigation_details.expected_result: Isolation of Staphylococcus aureus (or Streptococcus pyogenes)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Empirical therapy proceeds, but targeted therapy is missed if the organism is resistant.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms the causative organism and guides antibiotic choice — obtainable only in person, never via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound assessment with swab/culture at source control
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Same-day hands-on assessment establishes whether there is a drainable collection and provides the culture that guides therapy.
Relevant to future contraceptive planning once the implant is removed and fertility returns; not urgent to the acute infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine venous bloods / imaging in the absence of systemic-sepsis features
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required for a localised cellulitis with only early systemic features; reserve escalation to bloods/imaging for the sepsis or necrotising-infection pathway.
